summary: Comprehensive testing of GetLocal V2 Employer Authentication & SMS OTP verification. All 23 backend pytest tests passed. All frontend UI tests passed. Auth system is fully functional with route protection, JWT session management, and wallet binding to authenticated users.
passed_tests: POST /nextapi/auth/signup - Valid signup returns success with mock_hint
POST /nextapi/auth/verify-otp - Correct OTP (123456) creates employer + wallet + JWT
GET /nextapi/auth/me - Valid token returns employer profile
GET /nextapi/wallet - Works with valid auth (returns balance + unlockedCandidates)
E2E: Signup -> OTP Verify -> Buy Credits -> Unlock candidate (full flow)
UI: /hire redirects to /login when not authenticated
UI: /post-job redirects to /login when not authenticated
UI: Login with phone + password works
UI: Login redirects to /hire on success
UI: Wrong password shows error message
UI: Signup moves to OTP step
UI: Company name displayed in header after login
UI: Credits badge visible in header
UI: Logout clears session and redirects to /login
UI: Post Job button navigates to /post-job

mocked_integrations.SMS OTP: MOCKED - Always accepts OTP 123456. In production, replace with real SMS provider (e.g., Twilio, MSG91)
mocked_integrations.Razorpay Payment Gateway: MOCKED - Using placeholder keys. mock=true flag bypasses real payment checkout and adds credits directly

seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Used existing test accounts. New accounts created during E2E testing with unique phone numbers.
context_for_next_testing_agent:
Auth implementation is complete and fully tested. Key verified behaviors:
1. Signup requires phone, company_name, password (min 6 chars)
2. Signup stores pending registration in memory, awaiting OTP
3. OTP verification (123456 mock) creates employer in DB + wallet with 0 credits
4. JWT contains employer_id, phone, company_name - stored in localStorage as 'auth_token'
5. employer_id also stored in localStorage for backward compatibility
6. All protected routes (/nextapi/wallet, /nextapi/unlock, /nextapi/payments/*) require Bearer token
7. AuthProvider handles route protection client-side - redirects to /login if not authenticated
8. No 'default-employer' fallback - wallet is strictly bound to authenticated user
9. Backend test file: /app/getlocal/tests/test_auth_v1.py (23 tests)
10. Previous test files in /app/getlocal/tests/ cover other features (phase8_unlock, payments, matching)
